name: Roland - Turn 2 Cores into a 5 Character Pickup & Play Set
description_md: _-_ This is one of five decks designed to create as close possible to a pick up and play version of the base game from 2 core sets. The intention is that with all 5 constructed, up to 4 players can simply select the character of their choice needing only to transfer the following 6 cards from whichever char is not in play to any deck missing them: * 2x [Flashlight](/card/01087), * 2x [Knife](/card/01086), and * 2x [Emergency Cache](/card/01088) All other cards have been distributed among the five chars in such a way as to make legal decks which are functional, reasonably thematic, and each offer a different play style. Basically a rationalisation of the starter decks from the manual. Obviously this has required the odd compromise, so please do not expect these to be super strong decks or all choices to be perfectly thematic but ive done what I can. The other decks can be found here: - [Skids](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/6938/skids-turn-2-cores-into-a-5-character-pick-up-and-play-set-1.0) - [Wendy](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/6939/wendy-turn-2-cores-into-a-5-character-pick-up-and-play-set-1.0) - [Agnes](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/6940/agnes-turn-2-cores-into-a-5-character-pick-up-and-play-set-1.0) - [Daisy](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/6941/daisy-turn-2-cores-into-a-5-character-pick-up-and-play-set-1.0) These replace a previous set of decks based on the same concept that I wrote when the game was new and I had much less experience. The "playing as Roland" section is written to be a short summary to present to new players should you need it. _-_ **PLAYING AS ROLAND - THE FED** _This federal agent is diligent, combat trained and neurotic to the bone. While he has some talents at sleuthing, his preferred approach to clue gathering involves kicking down doors and interrogating cultists. With access to a wide range of weapons, including [his very own 38 special](/card/01006), and a high base he can pack a decent punch. But Roland thrives best where clues are to be found. With numerous cards feeding him them automatically and the ability to hoover them up as he fights, he can often get away with ignoring shroud or location restrictions. However behind that tough veneer lie Roland`s true motives. Protecting the public today is one thing, but some truths are so terrifying that preventing panic may require a [Cover Up](/card/01007)..._ * **Deck Complexity:.......Low** * **Clue Gathering:..........Moderate** * **Damage Dealing:........Excellent** * **Evasion:.......................Poor** * **Physical Defenses:.....High** * **Horror Defenses:.......Weak** _Muligan targets_: 1 weapon (not [Knife](/card/01086)) and a [Beat Cop](/card/01018) if possible. ***TIPS:*** **Money is going to be tight, prioritise combat first.** _First and foremost you are the teams damage dealer. Combat tools are expensive and Roland`s ability will enable you to get clues anyway. Avoid spending resources on investigation tools until you at least a weapon and ideally an ally down._ **[Evidence!](/card/01022) and [Working a Hunch](/card/01037) both have pips**. _With your base 3 this should be enough to get you a clue if you commit them to a test without needing to pay resources to play them on all but the toughest location._ **You are a damage dealer, not a tank.** _Your low sanity and moderate leave you vulnerable to horror, particularly from encounter card checks. Save your [Guts](/card/01089) and [Unexpected Courage](/card/01093) to protect you here, use [First Aid](/card/01019) on horror rather than damage, and dont be afraid to lose allies to horror shocks. Better them than you!_ **Other people are allowed to activate the on [Cover Up](/card/01007).** _ie they can clear it for you. You can help with any weakness or encounter cards with in another characters play area if they are at your location, you just cant use their good stuff like items unless it says otherwise. Dont worry about why for now. It is in the rules. Its just not always obvious when you first start out._ _-_ **SUGGESTED UPGRADES** _Priorities:_ * **[Beat Cop](/card/01018) [Beat Cop (2)](/card/01028)** - _Being able to keep that +1 in play and use the bonus automatic damage is a big deal in Roland._ _Options:_ * **[Police Badge (2)](/card/01027)** - _Did you just see [Daisy](/card/01002) taking an [Encyclopedia](/card/01042)? How does a 5 action turn look all of a sudden? Just be aware of its price and that [Skids](/card/01003) may have nicked it from your pocket..._ * **[Elder Sign Amulet (3)](/card/01095)** - _Its expensive but it sures up that sanity deficit potentially making tough enough to tank at last._ * **[Extra Ammunition (1)](/card/01026)** - _Essential if you want to take the [Shotgun](/card/01029) route, but even if not you do already have 3 guns in that deck (including [Your very own .45 Special](/card/01006))._ * **[Shotgun (4)](/card/01029) - I mean, it is a big gun...** _And it rarely misses. Notice how [Vicious Blow](/card/01025) potentially adds 2 damage, one from the pip. Non insignificant drawbacks include needing 2 hand slots and a small Ammunition pool [*cough](/card/01026). Its not easy to use, but lets face it thats not why you took it. Did I mention its a big gun?_ _-_ **EARLY CUTS (OR SWAP OUTS IF NO ONE TOOK SKIDS/DAISY):** * 2x **[Knife](/card/01086)** - you have many better weapons * 1x **[Physical Training](/card/01017)** - It can be a little expensive to use * 2x **[Flashlight](/card/01087)** - You have other ways (and people) to gather clues. While it works, this can be expensive and your handslots are often busy. * 1x [Manual Dexterity](/card/01092) _-_ ** NO ONE TOOK SKIDS ** If no one took [skids](/card/01003), feel free to swap in some of the following: * 1x [First Aid](/card/01019) * 2x [Perception](/card/01090) * 1x [Unexpected Courage](/card/01093) _-_ ** NO ONE TOOK DAISY** If no one took [Daisy](/card/01002), feel free to swap in some of the following: * 1x [Unexpected Courage](/card/01093) * 2x [Deduction](/card/01039) (its better than [Knife](/card/01086) here)
